rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component or implementation scopes
Place component type annexes in the component type scope and implementation annexes in the implementation scope
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or explicitly imported names
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope to maintain architectural clarity
Use only identifiers that are visible in the current scope or explicitly imported
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees that refer to component ports in implementation scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
